title: Implement Hardened Memory via Total Recall Orchestrator
description: Transition the memory management from best-effort asynchronous sync to a synchronous, guaranteed 'Memory Integrity Audit' using a local Total Recall orchestrator. This involves adding SessionEnd and Stop hooks and refactoring the SessionStart logic.

title: Implement idle baseline tracking with minimum time
description:
Implement automatic idle baseline tracking with a minimum time setting (default: 5 seconds).

Requirements:
1. Monitor CPU+GPU utilization in real-time
2. When combined < 5%, start a timer
3. Only record baseline if idle state persists for minimum time (default 5s)
4. Store multiple baseline readings in idle_baseline table
5. Calculate average baseline for more accurate readings
6. Configurable minimum idle time via config file

Use case: Prevents rapid toggling when system briefly dips below threshold during brief idle periods.

title: Create unit tests for cost calculator
description:
Create unit tests for electricity_cost.py covering:

1. Cost rate persistence and retrieval
2. Energy calculation (Wh to kWh to USD)
3. Session start/stop
4. Idle baseline calculation
5. Cumulative energy tracking

Use pytest and mock the database dependency.

title: Create unit tests for system metrics
description:
Create unit tests for system_metrics.py covering:

1. CPU metrics collection (psutil)
2. GPU metrics collection (NVML fallback to WMI)
3. Memory metrics collection
4. Per-process GPU tracking
5. Error handling when libraries unavailable

Use pytest and mock external dependencies.

title: Create unit tests for server metrics
description:
Create unit tests for server_metrics.py covering:

1. Metrics collection from /metrics endpoint
2. Slot parsing from /slots endpoint
3. Props parsing from /props endpoint
4. Error handling for network failures
5. Prometheus-format metrics parsing

Use pytest and mock the HTTP requests.

title: Create unit tests for database
description:
Create comprehensive unit tests for database.py covering:

1. Schema initialization
2. Insert operations (server, system, process GPU metrics)
3. Query operations with filters
4. Settings management (get/set)
5. Cost rate persistence
6. Idle baseline storage and retrieval
7. Cumulative energy tracking

Use pytest and mock external dependencies where needed.

title: Implement 1-hour compression function
description:
Implement a function to compress 1-minute metrics into 1-hour aggregates.

Function signature:


Aggregation rules (same as 1m compression):
- All numeric fields: AVG()
- Use timestamp truncated to hour boundary for grouping

Also compress server_metrics_1m -> server_metrics_1h using same rules.

title: Implement 1-minute compression function
description:
Implement a function to compress raw system metrics into 1-minute aggregates.

Function signature:


Aggregation rules:
- cpu_percent: AVG()
- cpu_temperature_c: AVG()
- cpu_power_w: AVG()
- gpu_usage: AVG()
- gpu_memory_used: AVG()
- gpu_temperature_c: AVG()
- gpu_fan_speed_rpm: AVG()
- gpu_power_w: AVG()
- memory_percent: AVG()
- system_power_w: AVG()

Use timestamp truncated to minute boundary for grouping.

title: Implement per-process CPU power calculation
description:
Implement per-process CPU power allocation: cpu_power_process = cpu_power_total * (cpu_percent_process / cpu_percent_total).

Need to:
1. Track total system CPU power and total CPU utilization
2. Calculate proportional allocation for each tracked process
3. Store per-process CPU power in database for cost tracking

title: Create data retention manager
description:
Create data retention manager that automatically compresses and purges old data based on configured retention policies.

Retention policy:
- Raw data: 1 week (default)
- 1-minute data: 1 month (default)
- 1-hour data: 1 year (default)
- Archive old data to separate file or compressed format
- Run VACUUM after purging to reclaim space

title: Implement data compression (raw -> 1m -> 1h)
description:
Implement data compression for system_metrics_raw into system_metrics_1m and system_metrics_1h tables. Use AVG() for rates and SUM() for counters. Add automatic retention management based on configured intervals.

Compression rules:
- raw (1-sec) -> 1m: AVG() for utilization/temperature/power, SUM() for counters
- 1m -> 1h: AVG() for rates, SUM() for cumulative counters
- Handle missing data gracefully (NULL values in aggregated data)

description: Create unit tests for compression logic covering: 1-minute bucket aggregation, 1-hour bucket aggregation, handling of empty data, and data gap handling in graphs.

description: Create unit tests for server_metrics.py covering: metrics endpoint parsing, slot parsing, collect() method, and error handling for unreachable server.

title: llama-monitor: Create unit tests for database
description: Create unit tests for database.py covering: schema initialization, metrics insertion, querying with filters, compression table operations, and settings management.

title: llama-monitor: Create data retention manager
description: Create data retention manager that automatically compresses and purges old data based on configured retention policies. Handle raw -> 1-minute -> 1-hour transitions and archive old data.

description: Create function to compress 1-minute metrics into 1-hour buckets. Aggregate using AVG() for rates and SUM() for counters. Store results in system_metrics_1h table.

description: Create function to compress raw system metrics into 1-minute buckets. Aggregate using AVG() for rates (cpu_percent, gpu_usage, etc.) and SUM() for counters. Store results in system_metrics_1m table.

Store multiple baseline readings and calculate average for more accurate baselines. Requires a minimum time setting (default: 5 seconds) to prevent rapid toggling when system briefly dips below threshold.

description: Implement per-process CPU power allocation: cpu_power_process = cpu_power_total * (cpu_percent_process / cpu_percent_total). Need to track total system power and calculate proportional allocation for tracked processes.

title: llama-monitor: Create web dashboard HTML
description: Create the web dashboard HTML template (templates/index.html) with Chart.js for smooth graphs. Include responsive layout, real-time updates via polling, and cost display toggle.

title: llama-monitor: Implement data compression
description: Implement data compression for system_metrics_raw into system_metrics_1m and system_metrics_1h tables. Use AVG() for rates and SUM() for counters. Add automatic retention management based on configured intervals.

title: llama-monitor: Create example config.yaml
description: Create example_config.yaml with all configurable options: server URL, polling_interval, compression levels, retention settings, display options, cost rate. Include comments explaining each option.

title: llama-monitor: Create README.md
description: Create README.md with: 1) Overview of llama-monitor, 2) Installation instructions, 3) Configuration guide, 4) Usage examples (TUI, web, CLI), 5) Architecture overview, 6) Troubleshooting section.

title: llama-monitor: Create requirements.txt
description: Create requirements.txt with all dependencies: requests, psutil, nvidia-ml-py, wmi, pyyaml, urwid/rich (TUI), flask (web), pytest (dev). Include version pins if needed.

title: llama-monitor: Create main entry point
description: Create main.py that orchestrates the monitor: 1) Parse CLI args (--server, --config, --polling-interval), 2) Load config.yaml, 3) Initialize database, 4) Start metrics collection loop with configurable interval, 5) Support TUI and web modes. Include graceful shutdown handling.

title: llama-monitor: Create CLI stats command
description: Create cli_stats.py with on-demand stats output: 1) Server stats (tokens/s, slots), 2) System stats (CPU, GPU, memory), 3) Cost for current session. Usage: python -m llama_monitor.cli_stats --server http://localhost:8000

title: llama-monitor: Create web server with WebSocket support
description: Create web_server.py with Flask + Flask-SocketIO: 1) GET / serves HTML dashboard with Chart.js, 2) GET /api/metrics/latest returns JSON from aggregator HTTP API, 3) GET /api/metrics/range for historical data, 4) GET /api/metrics/list for available metrics, 5) WebSocket for real-time client updates. Connects to aggregator daemon's HTTP API at localhost:8080.
design: Separate script architecture: web_server.py connects to aggregator_daemon.py via HTTP REST API. Uses Flask-SocketIO for WebSocket support. HTML dashboard includes real-time graphs for GPU/CPU usage, token rate, and power consumption. Includes session cost display.

title: llama-monitor: Create TUI dashboard
description: Create tui.py with curses-based terminal dashboard showing: 1) Server status (tokens, slots, progress), 2) System resources (CPU, GPU, memory), 3) Power & cost, 4) 60-second history graphs using Unicode blocks. Include controls: Q=quit, R=refresh, C=toggle cost, T=toggle temps.

title: llama-monitor: Create unit tests for cost calculator
description: Create tests/test_cost_calculator.py with tests for: 1) Basic cost calculation (energy_kwh, cost_usd), 2) Per-process cost calculation, 3) Idle baseline subtraction, 4) Edge cases (zero power, zero duration).

title: llama-monitor: Create unit tests for compression
description: Create tests/test_compression.py with tests for: 1) 1-minute bucket aggregation, 2) 1-hour bucket aggregation, 3) AVG vs SUM handling for different metrics, 4) Timestamp rounding, 5) Edge cases (empty data, single row).

title: llama-monitor: Create unit tests for database
description: Create tests/test_db.py with tests for: 1) Database initialization, 2) Table creation verification, 3) Insert operations, 4) Query operations, 5) Compression functions. Use in-memory SQLite for fast tests.

title: llama-monitor: Create unit tests for system metrics
description: Create tests/test_system_metrics.py with tests for: 1) CPU percentage collection via psutil, 2) GPU metrics via pynvml (mock if unavailable), 3) Memory metrics, 4) WMI fallback behavior. Mock external dependencies to ensure tests pass without GPU.

title: llama-monitor: Create unit tests for server metrics
description: Create tests/test_server_metrics.py with tests for: 1) Parsing /metrics endpoint (Prometheus format), 2) Parsing /slots endpoint, 3) Parsing /props endpoint, 4) Error handling when server unavailable, 5) Timestamp formatting. Use mock HTTP responses.

title: llama-monitor: Create configuration manager
description: Create config.py that loads config.yaml with validation. Include: server URL, polling_interval (1,2,5,10,15,20,30), compression levels (interval + start_after + unit), retention settings. Use pyyaml for parsing. Provide defaults for missing values.

title: llama-monitor: Create aggregator module
description: Create aggregator.py that orchestrates all metrics collection and storage. Functions: collect_all_metrics(), store_raw_metrics(), compress_if_needed(), calculate_cost(). Merge server + system + process metrics with timestamps. Store to SQLite with configurable retention.

title: llama-monitor: Create electricity cost calculator
description: Create electricity_cost.py with calculate_cost() function. Inputs: gpu_power_w, cpu_power_w, duration_seconds, cost_rate_usd_per_kwh. Output: energy_kwh, cost_usd. Include per-process cost calculation. Handle idle baseline subtraction for accurate inference power.

title: llama-monitor: Create data retention manager
description: Create retention_manager.py that manages data lifecycle. Implement: 1) Check if raw data should be compressed to 1-minute (after configured interval), 2) Check if 1-minute data should be compressed to 1-hour, 3) Run compression jobs based on schedule. Configurable via YAML (interval, start_after, unit).

title: llama-monitor: Create 1-hour compression function
description: Create compression.py with compress_to_1h() function that aggregates 1-minute metrics to 1-hour buckets. Use SQL AVG() for rates, SUM() for counters. Round timestamps to hour boundaries. Return count of compressed rows.

title: llama-monitor: Create 1-minute compression function
description: Create compression.py with compress_to_1m() function that aggregates raw metrics to 1-minute buckets. Use SQL AVG() for rates (cpu_percent, gpu_usage), SUM() for counters (prompt_tokens_total). Round timestamps to minute boundaries. Return count of compressed rows.

title: llama-monitor: Create raw metrics inserter
description: Create metrics_inserter.py with functions to insert raw metrics into database: insert_server_metrics_raw(), insert_system_metrics_raw(), insert_process_gpu_metrics_raw(). Use parameterized queries. Return success/failure status. Include basic validation of input data.

title: llama-monitor: Create SQLite database initializer
description: Create db.py that initializes SQLite database with all tables (server_metrics_raw/_1m/_1h, system_metrics_raw/_1m/_1h, process_gpu_metrics_raw/_1m/_1h, idle_baseline, cumulative_energy). Include migration support for schema changes. Provide functions: init_db(), get_connection(), close_connection(). Use context manager for safe connection handling.

title: llama-monitor: Create cumulative energy counter
description: Create cumulative_energy_counter.py to track ever-increasing Wh consumed values. Track: total_wh, gpu_wh, cpu_wh, session_cost_usd. Store in cumulative_energy table (single row with id=1). Update on each metrics collection cycle. Calculate Wh = (power_w * seconds) / 3600. Reset on session start.

title: llama-monitor: Create idle baseline tracker
description: Create idle_baseline_tracker.py that monitors combined CPU+GPU utilization. When both < 5% simultaneously, record system power as idle baseline to idle_baseline table. Store timestamp, cpu_percent, gpu_percent, system_power_w, is_valid flag. Implement algorithm to detect idle periods and store occasional data efficiently.

title: llama-monitor: Create per-process GPU metrics collector
description: Create process_gpu_metrics.py using NVML's nvmlDeviceGetGraphicsRunningProcesses_v2() to get per-process GPU utilization. Match llama-server.exe by process name, extract pid, gpu_utilization, gpu_memory_mb. Store in process_gpu_metrics_raw table. Fallback to WMI if NVML unavailable. Handle process restarts by tracking PID changes.

title: llama-monitor: Create system metrics collector
description: Create system_metrics.py using psutil (CPU, memory) and pynvml (GPU usage, memory, temp, power). Collect: cpu_percent, cpu_cores_percent (list), cpu_temperature_c (list), cpu_power_w, gpu_usage, gpu_memory_used/total, gpu_temperature_c, gpu_fan_speed_rpm, gpu_power_w, memory_used/total/percent, system_power_w. Include WMI fallback with warning if NVML unavailable. Return structured Python dict with timestamp.

title: llama-monitor: Create server metrics collector
description: Create server_metrics.py that fetches metrics from llama.cpp server endpoints: /metrics (Prometheus format), /slots (slot states with progress), /props (server config). Parse /metrics to extract: prompt_tokens_total, prompt_tokens_seconds, tokens_predicted_total, predicted_tokens_seconds, requests_processing, requests_deferred. Return structured Python dict with timestamp, server metrics, and slots list. Include error handling for server unavailable.

title: llama-monitor: Design database schema
description: Create SQLite database schema for llama-monitor with all tables: server_metrics_raw/_1m/_1h, system_metrics_raw/_1m/_1h, process_gpu_metrics_raw/_1m/_1h, idle_baseline, and cumulative_energy. Each table should have appropriate columns based on the design document. This is a design task - create schema SQL file with comments explaining each table's purpose.

title: llama-monitor: Idle baseline tracking
description: Implement idle baseline power consumption tracking. The system should: 1) Monitor combined CPU + GPU utilization 2) When both < 5% simultaneously, record system power as idle baseline 3) Store in idle_baseline table with is_valid flag 4) Calculate actual inference power: power_inference = power_total - power_idle. This allows accurate cost calculation for llama-server.exe even when background processes are running.
notes: Superseded by: imperium-astrale-3b0 (idle baseline tracker). Original task is now decomposed into smaller, testable chunks.

title: llama-monitor: Per-process GPU monitoring
description: Implement per-process GPU utilization tracking using NVML's nvmlDeviceGetGraphicsRunningProcesses_v2() to identify llama-server.exe's GPU usage and calculate its power consumption separately from other processes (e.g., video games). The implementation should: 1) Use pynvml to get running graphics processes 2) Match llama-server.exe by process name 3) Calculate process power: gpu_power_process = gpu_power_total * (gpu_util_process / gpu_util_total) 4) Store per-process metrics in process_gpu_metrics_raw table 5) Handle process restarts by tracking PID changes 6) Fallback to WMI if NVML unavailable (with warning)
notes: Superseded by: imperium-astrale-3bx (per-process GPU metrics collector). Original task is now decomposed into smaller, testable chunks.

title: llama-monitor: TUI + Web dashboard
description: Implement both TUI (terminal) and Web (Flask) dashboards sharing the same data layer. TUI uses ASCII charts (60-second history on single line). Web uses Chart.js for smooth graphs. Both show server metrics, system resources, power draw, cost, and temperatures.
notes: Superseded by: imperium-astrale-ud2 (TUI), imperium-astrale-9iu (web dashboard). Original task is now decomposed into smaller, testable chunks.

title: Add breadcrumb validation to memory audits
description: Add validation to check that memory breadcrumbs are properly linked across layers. Why: Orphaned memories and broken links reduce recall effectiveness. What to do: 1) Check every JRN_ entry has at least one breadcrumb link, 2) Check every REM_ entry links to relevant JRN_ entries, 3) Check every Beads issue linked still exists, 4) Report broken links in audit. Acceptance Criteria: Audit validates breadcrumbs and reports broken links. Type: task, Priority: 4

title: Add conditional save debounce for PostToolUse hook
description: Implement smart debouncing to prevent excessive saves during active editing. Why: Without debouncing, every Edit/Write triggers a save, causing noise and latency. What to do: 1) Track last tool use timestamp, 2) Wait 30-60s after last tool use before saving, 3) Track per-file or global changes, 4) Only save if significant changes detected. Design Notes: Debounce window 30-60s, track by file or globally. Acceptance Criteria: PostToolUse debounces saves during active editing. Type: task, Priority: 4

title: Review and document Remember plugin upgrade strategy
description: Document the strategy for handling Remember plugin updates to avoid repeated manual changes. Why: The Remember plugin is not ours and updates require manual tracking of custom changes. What to do: 1) Identify all custom modifications to Remember plugin scripts, 2) Document upgrade procedure, 3) Create automated diff checker for new versions, 4) Consider fork vs patch strategy. Acceptance Criteria: Upgrade procedure documented, diff checker created, strategy approved. Type: task, Priority: 4

title: Improve emergency capture with actual session extraction
description: Improve emergency capture to extract actual session state rather than just a static template. Why: Current emergency capture creates a static template with no actual session data. What to do: 1) Extract recent exchanges from session transcript, 2) Capture current file states, 3) Link to existing memories, 4) Include open Beads and pending tasks. Design Notes: Use session transcript API, include file diff snapshots. Acceptance Criteria: Emergency capture includes recent session activity, file states, and links to existing memories. Type: task, Priority: 3

title: Add memory health metrics to audit reports
description: Add metrics to track memory system health and identify decay over time. Why: The current audit reports pass/fail but doesn't track measurable metrics to identify slow decay or measure improvement. What to do: 1) Track days since last memory capture, 2) Track open Beads without corresponding REM_ entries, 3) Track JRN_ entries lacking breadcrumbs, 4) Add percentage metrics, 5) Report metrics in audit output as Health Score. Design Notes: Metrics should be lightweight, use simple thresholds, health score 100=ideal. Acceptance Criteria: Audit shows days since capture, beads without links, orphaned JRN entries, health score. Type: task, Priority: 3

title: Add Injection Phase verification after SessionStart recovery
description:
Add verification that the Remember plugin's recovery phase completed successfully before the session proceeds.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md specifies an 'Injection Phase' where updated memory state should be injected into the current session context. Currently, Total Recall runs recovery/consolidation but doesn't verify they completed or inject the memory into Claude's context.

## What to do
1. Add verification check for recovery completion (check for last-save.json marker)
2. Add verification check for consolidation completion (check for last-ndc.ts marker)
3. Report verification status in audit output
4. If verification fails, log warning but don't block session (fail-soft for robustness)

## Design Notes
- The Remember plugin runs recovery/consolidation in background
- We need to verify these completed before proceeding
- Should be fail-soft (warn but don't block) to avoid blocking sessions

## Acceptance Criteria
- SessionStart audit verifies recovery completed
- SessionStart audit verifies consolidation completed
- Verification status is reported in audit output

## Dependencies
- This blocks: nothing (standalone improvement)

title: Add PostToolUse hook with smart debouncing for significant tool use
description:
Add the PostToolUse hook to Total Recall, which was specified in MEMORY_RULES.md but never implemented.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md (line 71) specifies a PostToolUse hook: '(Optional) Provides a subtle nudge to verify/record recent decisions.' This hook was never added to hooks.json or memory_sync.py.

## What to do
1. Add PostToolUse hook entry to .claude/skills/total-recall/hooks.json
2. Add --post-tool command-line flag to memory_sync.py
3. Implement post_tool_use_mode() function to handle the event
4. The debounce logic is handled in imperium-astrale-eml

## Design Notes
- This is marked as optional in the spec
- Smart debouncing is critical to avoid noise during active editing
- Should only trigger on significant tool use, not every minor edit

## Acceptance Criteria
- PostToolUse hook is registered in hooks.json
- memory_sync.py handles --post-tool flag
- Handoff digest or journal entry is created after tool use (if significant)

## Dependencies
- Blocked by: nothing
- Blocks: imperium-astrale-eml (debounce logic)
notes: Note: This is the primary issue for PostToolUse implementation. The original issue imperium-astrale-u62 should be marked as duplicate/closed.

title: Add PostToolUse hook with smart debouncing for significant tool use
description:
Add the PostToolUse hook to Total Recall, which was specified in MEMORY_RULES.md but never implemented.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md (line 71) specifies a PostToolUse hook: '(Optional) Provides a subtle nudge to verify/record recent decisions.' This hook was never added to hooks.json or memory_sync.py.

## What to do
1. Add PostToolUse hook entry to .claude/skills/total-recall/hooks.json
2. Add --post-tool command-line flag to memory_sync.py
3. Implement post_tool_use_mode() function to handle the event
4. Add smart debouncing to only trigger on significant changes (e.g., file size change > threshold, or wait 30s after last tool use)
5. Group related tool uses (e.g., multiple edits to same file within debounce window)

## Design Notes
- This is marked as optional in the spec
- Smart debouncing is critical to avoid noise during active editing
- Should only trigger on significant tool use, not every minor edit
- Consider tracking 'last tool use' timestamp per file or globally

## Acceptance Criteria
- PostToolUse hook is registered in hooks.json
- memory_sync.py handles --post-tool flag
- Debounce logic prevents excessive saves during active editing
- Handoff digest or journal entry is created after tool use (if significant)

## Dependencies
- This blocks: imperium-astrale-u62 (the original PostToolUse issue which should be marked as duplicate/blocked)
close_reason: Superseded by imperium-astrale-hdx which has more complete requirements

Database schema updated with process_gpu_metrics_* and idle_baseline tables. See DESIGN.md for full details.

title: Update memory_sync.py to use auto-generated IDs
description:
Update memory_sync.py to use the new ID generation helper function instead of inline ID creation.

## Why
The current code creates IDs inline in multiple places:
- handoff_mode():  (line 402)
- emergency_capture_mode(): timestamp-based filename generation

This defeats the purpose of the helper function and keeps the LLM burden high.

## What to do
1. Import or define the ID generation helper function
2. Replace all inline ID generation with calls to the helper
3. The helper should ONLY require: layer prefix + slug
4. Timestamp and full ID construction happen server-side

## Acceptance Criteria
- No inline f-string ID generation remains in memory_sync.py
- Helper function is called for all ID creation
- Tests show IDs are correctly generated with valid timestamps

title: Create ID generation helper function for memory entries
description:
Create a centralized helper function to generate memory entry IDs with the format [Layer]_[YYYYMMDD]_[HHMMSS]_[slug].

## Why
The current memory_sync.py generates IDs inline (e.g., ). This approach has problems:
- LLMs cannot reliably generate timestamps across 128k+ context windows
- Timestamps like 20260525_999999 are invalid
- Slugs may be misspelled, breaking the breadcrumb chain

## What to do
1. Create a Python helper function in memory_sync.py (or a new utils.py) that:
   - Takes: layer (REM/JRN), slug (string), optional content
   - Auto-generates: timestamp (YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS), full ID
   - Returns: full ID string
   - Validates: slug format, timestamp validity
2. Update all ID generation calls to use this helper
3. Document the function for future use

## Design Decision
Store this in memory_sync.py for now since it's tightly coupled to the sync logic. If it grows, we can split to utils.py later.

description:
Add the PostToolUse hook to Total Recall, which was specified in MEMORY_RULES.md but never implemented.

## Why
The MEMORY_RULES.md (line 71) specifies a PostToolUse hook: "(Optional) Provides a subtle nudge to verify/record recent decisions." This hook was never added to hooks.json or memory_sync.py.

## What to do
